---
title: "CPS Resource Allocation Plot"
subtitle: "Regression Analysis of the Resource Index (RI) and Opportunity Index (OI) Across CPS Schools"
author:
  - name: "Andi Fang"
  - name: "Minyang Xu"
date: "2026-02-25"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
        \fvset{breaklines=true,breakanywhere=true}
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Regression Analysis: RI and OI

In this section, we examine the relationship between the **Resource Index (RI)** and the **Opportunity Index (OI)**.

Specifically, we estimate the following linear model:

RIᵢ = α + β OIᵢ + εᵢ

This regression allows us to assess whether schools serving more disadvantaged populations (higher OI) tend to have systematically different resource levels (RI).

---

# Environment Setup for Analysis

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import altair as alt
from pathlib import Path
import statsmodels.api as sm
import tempfile
from IPython.display import Image, display
import vl_convert as vlc
import os

# Define project paths robustly for Quarto
project_root = Path(os.environ.get("QUARTO_PROJECT_DIR", Path.cwd())).resolve().parent
derived_dir = project_root / "data" / "derived-data"

# Load cleaned dataset
df = pd.read_csv(derived_dir / "cps_df.csv")
```

We load the previously constructed dataset containing RI and OI.

---

# Data Cleaning for Regression

```{python}
# Drop missing values in key variables
df_clean = df.dropna(subset=['OI', 'RI'])

len(df_clean)
```

We restrict the regression sample to schools with non-missing Resource Index and Opportunity Index values.

---

# Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) Regression

```{python}
# Define regression variables
X = sm.add_constant(df_clean['OI'])
y = df_clean['RI']

# Fit OLS model
model = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()

# Extract p-value for OI coefficient
p_value = model.pvalues['OI']
p_text = f"p-value: {p_value:.3e}"
slope = model.params[1]

model.summary()
```

This regression estimates the marginal association between OI and RI.  
The p-value indicates whether the relationship is statistically significant.

---

# Visualization: RI vs OI

To better illustrate the relationship, we construct a scatter plot with:

- Individual schools as points
- A fitted linear regression line
- The p-value displayed on the figure

```{python}
# Scatter Plot

scatter = alt.Chart(df_clean).mark_point(
    filled=True,
    size=75,
    opacity=0.75,
    color="#1f77b4"
).encode(
    x=alt.X(
        'OI:Q',
        title='Opportunity Index (Structural Disadvantage)',
        axis=alt.Axis(labelFontSize=12, titleFontSize=14)
    ),
    y=alt.Y(
        'RI:Q',
        title='Resource Index (Hard + Soft School Resources)',
        axis=alt.Axis(labelFontSize=12, titleFontSize=14)
    ),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip('Short_Name:N', title='School'),
        alt.Tooltip('OI:Q', title='Opportunity Index', format=".2f"),
        alt.Tooltip('RI:Q', title='Resource Index', format=".2f")
    ]
)

# Regression Line

regression = scatter.transform_regression(
    'OI', 'RI'
).mark_line(
    color='black',
    size=2
)

# Annotation (Slope + p-value)

annotation_text = f"Slope = {slope:.2f} (p = {p_value:.3f})"

annotation = alt.Chart(
    pd.DataFrame({'text': [annotation_text]})
).mark_text(
    align='right',
    baseline='bottom',
    dx=-10,
    dy=-10,
    fontSize=11,
    color='gray'
).encode(
    x=alt.value(640),
    y=alt.value(440),
    text='text:N'
)

# Combine Layers

chart = (scatter + regression + annotation).properties(
    width=700,
    height=480,
    title={
        "text": "Do Higher-Need Schools Receive More Resources?",
        "subtitle": [
            "Opportunity Index measures structural disadvantage",
            "Resource Index combines facility utilization and 5Essentials survey scores"
        ],
        "fontSize": 18,
        "subtitleFontSize": 12
    }
)

chart
```

The scatter plot visually represents the direction and strength of the relationship between school disadvantage and resource allocation.

---

# Exporting High-Resolution PNG

For submission purposes, we export the figure as a high-resolution PNG file.

```{python}
# Convert chart to PNG bytes
png_bytes = vlc.vegalite_to_png(chart.to_dict(), scale=3)

# Save to derived-data directory
output_path = derived_dir / "plot_RI_vs_OI.png"
with open(output_path, "wb") as f:
    f.write(png_bytes)

print(f"Saved chart as PNG: {output_path}")
```

The image file can now be used in slides or included in the PDF submission.

---

# Interpretation

If β > 0:
Schools serving more disadvantaged populations tend to have higher Resource Index values.

If β < 0:
Schools serving more disadvantaged populations tend to have lower Resource Index values.

Statistical significance (based on the p-value) determines whether this relationship is distinguishable from zero.

This analysis provides a first step toward evaluating whether resource allocation systematically aligns with student disadvantage.
